Seminole County FHA Loans

FHA loans in Seminole County, Florida

FHA loans across Seminole County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, why these established Orlando suburbs suit FHA buyers, and how to get pre-approved.

Seminole County Home Loans

FHA loans in Seminole County, Florida

Just north of Orlando, Seminole County is known for its established, family-friendly suburbs and well-regarded schools — places like Lake Mary, Oviedo, and Winter Springs. It's a more built-out, mature market than the booming counties nearby, with a mix of single-family homes and townhomes. The FHA loan limit covers most Seminole homes, making 3.5% down a practical path for families and first-time buyers.

Seminole County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Seminole County

With the 2026 FHA floor of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Seminole County home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). Across most of Seminole County, that covers the large majority of homes. A handful of Florida counties are set higher, so we confirm Seminole County's exact figure with HUD.

Established Orlando suburbs and FHA

Seminole's appeal is its schools and settled neighborhoods, drawing families willing to compete for move-in-ready homes. Single-family homes are straightforward FHA purchases; for townhomes and condos, confirm the building/association is FHA-approved via our condo guide. It's a strong county for first-time buyers who want Orlando access with a suburban feel. Older homes can use an FHA 203(k) loan.

Communities We Serve

FHA loans across Seminole County

We help FHA buyers throughout Seminole County, including Sanford, Altamonte Springs, Lake Mary, Oviedo, Winter Springs, and Casselberry. Wherever you are in the county, we will explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment, and get you pre-approved fast. Start with our first-time buyer guide or estimate your payment with the FHA calculator.

Down payment help in Seminole County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Seminole County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Seminole County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Seminole County for 2026?+
Seminole County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most homes in the county. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Seminole County good for FHA buyers with families?+
Yes — its well-regarded schools and established suburbs make it a strong family market, and the FHA limit covers most homes.
Can I buy a Seminole County townhome with FHA?+
Yes, if the building/association is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. We verify before you offer.
How much down payment do I need in Seminole County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Seminole County?+
Yes — Sanford, Altamonte Springs, Lake Mary, Oviedo, Winter Springs, Casselberry, and every community in the county.
